title: View, filter, and search optimization
target: Views, filters, search helper fields, and operator-facing table navigation
done_criteria: Each authority/operational table has proposed views and search/filter helpers tied to real workflows.
validation_notes: Current baseline shows all 21 tables only have Grid view; view optimization must be explicit and table-specific.
context: This workstream improves daily usability without deleting data.

title: Define view inclusion criteria by table role
target: All non-Delete Queue authority and operational tables
done_criteria: For each table role, define which views are needed: active, review, blocked, stale, delete_candidate, by area/type/status, recent, and validation/evidence views.
validation_notes: Do not create views that duplicate Grid view without adding filter/sort value.
context: Views should support operator navigation and reduce broad searches.

title: Create table-specific view proposal packet
target: Proposed views and filter/sort definitions
done_criteria: Packet lists table, view name, purpose, filter fields, sort fields, required helper fields, and whether API/manual creation is needed.
validation_notes: Pause for approval before schema/view work. If Airtable API cannot create required view type, produce manual instructions.
context: View creation may require manual Airtable work depending on API support.

title: Optimize search text and primary display behavior
target: Primary fields, search_text helpers, normalized display keys
done_criteria: Identify tables where search misses likely because useful terms are buried in long text or non-primary fields; propose helper fields or display conventions.
validation_notes: Approval packet must show examples of current search pain and proposed improved searchable values.
context: Search optimization should favor operator-facing clarity and stable keys.

title: Verify views/search/filter improvements
target: Post-view/helper readback and sample search checks
done_criteria: Verify created views/helper fields exist and table navigation/search examples work as expected.
validation_notes: Completion requires readback or manual screenshot-free evidence where API cannot inspect views fully.
context: Do not claim usability improvement without demonstrating the changed navigation/search path.

title: Authority-table simplification
target: Governance Control Plane, Queue Control, Plans, Work Items, WBS, Admin Registry, Lifecycle Ledger
done_criteria: Responsibilities are simplified and documented so each authority table has one clear purpose and no conflicting current-state claim.
validation_notes: No migration/delete of authority tables until a decision packet proves better structure and dependencies.
context: Airtable remains live operational authority; GitHub remains source/readback/tooling authority.

title: Define table responsibility matrix
target: Authority and operational tables
done_criteria: Matrix states purpose, source authority role, write owner, readback use, what does not belong there, and relationship to other tables.
validation_notes: Must resolve overlap between Plans/Work Items/WBS/Admin Registry/Lifecycle Ledger without deleting tables prematurely.
context: This answers how existing Plans/Work Items fit into the redesigned model.

title: Identify duplicated authority claims
target: Rows/fields that claim active state, current plan, queue order, or source authority
done_criteria: Report lists duplicate/conflicting authority claims and recommends keep/update/archive per claim.
validation_notes: Use live Airtable readback; do not use old GitHub CP files as current state authority unless explicitly doing source comparison.
context: Goal is fewer conflicting sources of truth.

title: Stage authority simplification packet
target: Authority rows/fields requiring update/archive
done_criteria: Packet includes exact row/field updates, before values, proposed values, rationale, and dependency/rollback notes.
validation_notes: Pause before writes. High-impact changes to authority rows require explicit approval.
context: Authority simplification is data mutation, not just documentation.

title: Verify authority simplification readback
target: Post-update authority readback
done_criteria: Verify no conflicting active branch/current plan/source authority claim remains after approved updates.
validation_notes: Completion requires readback from affected authority tables.
context: Authority changes must be verified before closeout or checkpoint.

title: Plans, Work Items, and WBS model decision
target: Plans table, Work Items table, WBS table model
done_criteria: A clear decision is made: retain, integrate, replace, or migrate each table role with exact evidence and migration path.
validation_notes: Current default is retain Plans as parent scope, Work Items as active queue leaves, and WBS as decomposed execution scaffold until evidence supports migration.
context: Do not prefix Plans or Work Items for deletion now. That would be premature and high risk.

title: Evaluate current Plans role
target: Plans table
done_criteria: Assess whether Plans should remain parent-scope authority, what fields should be simplified, and whether active_task fields should link to WBS/Work Items or be retired.
validation_notes: Known blank candidates such as active_plan_task_id must be evaluated with dependency evidence, not deleted from blank count alone.
context: Recommendation before evidence: keep Plans, simplify fields, and link or reference WBS more cleanly.

title: Evaluate current Work Items role
target: Work Items table
done_criteria: Assess whether Work Items should remain active queue leaf authority, which fields need select normalization/helper IDs, and whether parent_plan references should become linked/select/helper fields.
validation_notes: Do not migrate or delete Work Items unless a replacement active queue model is approved and tested.
context: Recommendation before evidence: keep Work Items as executable queue leaves.

title: Evaluate WBS integration model
target: Generic WBS table and relationships to Plans/Work Items
done_criteria: Define whether WBS rows are child decomposition under Plans, whether Work Items link to WBS leaves, and what status/gate fields should synchronize or remain independent.
validation_notes: Output must be an explicit relationship model with examples.
context: WBS should not become a second live queue unless intentionally designed.

title: Prepare migration-or-integration decision packet
target: Plans/Work Items/WBS model decision
done_criteria: Packet states recommended model, exact table/field changes, migration records if any, reference updates, and what remains unchanged.
validation_notes: Explicit approval required before any migration, prefix, or retirement action.
context: This is where any decision to migrate away from old Plans/Work Items would occur, not now.

title: DCOIR collector and Gemini deliverable support
target: Collector, Gemini, validation, workflow, skill, and packaging support fields/views
done_criteria: Database redesign explicitly supports current collector/Gemini deliverables and does not optimize Airtable in isolation.
validation_notes: Consult relevant validation/test/reference tables before recommendations.
context: Primary objective includes DCOIR collector / Gemini agent deliverables.

title: Map deliverable support requirements
target: Validation Test Cases, Validation Evidence, Gemini Research Reference, Work Items, Operator Tools Registry
done_criteria: Identify what fields/views/helpers are required to support collector/Gemini planning, testing, evidence, tool selection, and delivery closeout.
validation_notes: Do not assume requirements from memory alone; use Airtable readback and current active deliverable context.
context: This ties schema cleanup to operational usefulness.

title: Stage deliverable-support field/view packet
target: Fields/views/helpers supporting collector/Gemini lanes
done_criteria: Packet lists exact additions/updates and explains which deliverable workflow each supports.
validation_notes: Pause before writes; avoid adding fields that are not tied to a deliverable or recurring workflow.
context: Operational usefulness is mandatory for new structure.

title: Execution waves and approval packets
target: Wave A/B/C/D execution sequence and approval templates
done_criteria: Redesign implementation is grouped into explicit waves with approval packet structure, stop conditions, and validation artifacts.
validation_notes: No destructive/high-impact action occurs without exact target/action/payload approval.
context: This keeps the aggressive cleanup stance controlled and auditable.

title: Define Wave A non-destructive schema helpers/views
target: Safe helper/select/ID/dedupe/view additions
done_criteria: Wave A packet contains only additive/non-destructive changes with exact create/update payloads and readback gates.
validation_notes: Approval required before execution even if non-destructive.
context: Wave A should improve structure without deleting or overwriting existing data.

title: Define Wave B controlled-select normalization and backfill
target: Approved value normalization and safe backfills
done_criteria: Wave B packet contains value maps, target records, before-value gates, and after-readback checks.
validation_notes: Use chatgpt-exec for bulk execution and artifact inspection.
context: Wave B is data mutation but not destructive deletion.

title: Define Wave C destructive retirement actions
target: Field/table retirement, DELETE-prefix, deletion, duplicate merge/delete candidates
done_criteria: Wave C packet lists exact destructive/high-impact targets and waits for explicit approval immediately before execution.
validation_notes: Separate approval for prefix/retire vs actual deletion. Preserve Delete Queue semantics for row deletion only.
context: This is the highest-risk wave and must be isolated.

title: Define Wave D validation, checkpoint, and closeout
target: Final readback, metrics, validation evidence, checkpoint
done_criteria: Wave D verifies final schema/data/view state, records metrics, creates evidence/checkpoint, and identifies remaining follow-up work.
validation_notes: Closeout incomplete without durable checkpoint unless operator says not to checkpoint.
context: Readback and continuity are required after redesign waves.

title: Create approval packet template
target: Approval packet standard for all high-impact actions
done_criteria: Template requires exact target, action, before state, payload, expected after state, risk, rollback/stop conditions, validation method, and explicit operator approval line.
validation_notes: Template must be used before destructive/high-impact actions.
context: This operationalizes the pause-before-mutation rule.
